page1.grammarTopic: Past Simple — storytelling with sequence words
page1.grammarExplanation: In storytelling, we use Past Simple with SEQUENCE WORDS to connect events and make stories flow naturally. Sequence words: First (эхлээд), Then (дараа нь), After that (үүний дараа), Next (дараачийн), Suddenly (гэнэт), Finally (эцэст нь), At first (эхэндээ), Later (хожим). These words tell the listener WHEN and IN WHAT ORDER things happened.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Түүх ярихдаа Past Simple-г ДАРААЛАЛЫН ҮГС-тэй хослуулдаг. Дараалалын үгс: First (эхлээд), Then (дараа нь), After that (үүний дараа), Suddenly (гэнэт), Finally (эцэст нь), At first (эхэндээ), Later (хожим). Эдгээр үгс үйл явдлуудын ДАРААЛАЛыг харуулдаг.
commonMistakes: ❌ First I go to school.  ✅ First I WENT to school.
❌ Then suddenly I am happy.  ✅ Then suddenly I WAS happy.
❌ After that I move.  ✅ After that I MOVED.
❌ Finally I pass.  ✅ Finally I PASSED.

page2.readingPassage: Let me tell you my life story. I was born in Ulaanbaatar in 2008. I grew up in a small apartment near the city centre. At first, life was simple and very happy. When I was six, I started primary school. At first, English was very difficult for me. But then I found a wonderful teacher. After that, I practised English every single day. Suddenly, I realised I could actually speak it! Finally, English became my favourite subject. Now I am telling my story in English — and that makes me incredibly proud!

page1.grammarTopic: Present Perfect — have/has been (life experience)
page1.grammarExplanation: The Present Perfect (have/has + past participle) describes LIFE EXPERIENCES — things you have done at some unspecified time in your life, without saying exactly WHEN. Compare: 'I have been to Seoul.' (experience, no specific time) vs 'I went to Seoul last year.' (specific time = use Past Simple!). Signal words: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, before, once, twice.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Present Perfect (have/has + past participle) нь АМЬДРАЛЫН ТУРШЛАГА-г тодорхой цагийг заалгүй илэрхийлдэг. Харьцуул: 'I have been to Seoul.' (туршлага, цаг заагаагүй) VS 'I went to Seoul last year.' (тодорхой цаг = Past Simple хэрэглэ!). Дохио үгс: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently.
commonMistakes: ❌ I have been to Seoul last year.  ✅ I WENT to Seoul last year. (specific time!)
❌ Have you been there yesterday?  ✅ Did you go there yesterday?
❌ I have never went there.  ✅ I have never BEEN there.
❌ Has she went to Japan?  ✅ Has she BEEN to Japan?

Teacher: Have you ever been to Europe?
Sarnai: No, I haven't been to Europe yet. But it's definitely on my list!

page1.grammarTopic: Present Perfect — have/has + past participle (eaten/seen/met/done)
page1.grammarExplanation: Present Perfect uses HAVE/HAS + PAST PARTICIPLE (the 3rd form of the verb). Regular past participles add -ed (studied, visited, cooked). Irregular past participles must be memorized: eat→eaten, see→seen, do→done, go→gone, write→written, speak→spoken, ride→ridden, fly→flown, break→broken, forget→forgotten, win→won. This describes experiences without saying the exact time.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Present Perfect нь HAVE/HAS + PAST PARTICIPLE (3-р хэлбэр) ашигладаг. Ердийн үйл үгс: -ed нэм (studied, visited). Тэгдэггүй үйл үгс: цээжлэх ёстой. eat→eaten, see→seen, do→done, go→gone, write→written, speak→spoken, ride→ridden, fly→flown, break→broken, forget→forgotten. Яг ХЭЗЭЭ болсныг заалгүй туршлагаа хэлдэг.
commonMistakes: ❌ I have ate sushi.  ✅ I have EATEN sushi.
❌ She has saw that movie.  ✅ She has SEEN that movie.
❌ Have you ever did that?  ✅ Have you ever DONE that?
❌ He has went to Korea.  ✅ He has GONE to Korea.

page1.grammarTopic: Present Perfect with for and since — talking about duration
page1.grammarExplanation: Use FOR with a PERIOD OF TIME (how long): for 3 years, for a month, for ages, for a long time. Use SINCE with a POINT IN TIME (start): since 2010, since Monday, since I was born, since last year. These go with Present Perfect when the situation continues to NOW: 'I have studied English for 30 days.' (started 30 days ago, still true now!)
page1.grammarExplanationMn: FOR нь ХУГАЦААНЫ УРТЫГ (хэр удаан) илэрхийлдэг: for 3 years, for a month, for ages. SINCE нь ЭХЛЭХ ЦЭГийг илэрхийлдэг: since 2010, since Monday, since last year. Эдгээрийг Present Perfect-тэй хэрэглэдэг — нөхцөл байдал ОДОО хүртэл үргэлжилж байгааг харуулна.

page1.grammarTopic: Present Perfect vs Past Simple — when to use each tense
page1.grammarExplanation: KEY RULE: Use PRESENT PERFECT when there is NO specific time (experience, recent events, continuing situations). Use PAST SIMPLE when there IS a specific time (yesterday, last year, in 2020, when I was 6). Signal words for Present Perfect: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, for, since. Signal words for Past Simple: yesterday, last week, ago, in [year], when.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ГОЛ ДҮРЭМ: PRESENT PERFECT — тодорхой цаг БАЙХГҮЙ үед (туршлага, саяхан болсон зүйл, үргэлжилж буй нөхцөл байдал). PAST SIMPLE — тодорхой цаг БАЙГАА үед (yesterday, last year, in 2020, when I was 6). PP дохио үгс: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, for, since. PS дохио үгс: yesterday, last week, ago, in [year].
commonMistakes: ❌ I have been there last year.  ✅ I WENT there last year. (specific!)
❌ Did you ever eat sushi?  ✅ HAVE you ever eaten sushi?
❌ When have you gone?  ✅ WHEN DID you go? (when = Past Simple!)
❌ I lived here since 2010.  ✅ I HAVE LIVED here since 2010.

When we say WHEN, we use Past Simple. When there is NO specific time, we use Present Perfect. Now you understand both tenses!

page1.grammarTopic: Comparative adjectives — taller, smarter, more beautiful
page1.grammarExplanation: Comparative adjectives compare TWO people or things. SHORT adjectives (1-2 syllables): add -ER + than → tall→taller, smart→smarter, fast→faster. Irregular spelling: big→bigger (double), nice→nicer (-e). LONG adjectives (3+ syllables): use MORE + adjective + than → more beautiful, more intelligent, more expensive. IRREGULAR: good→better, bad→worse, far→further.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Харьцуулах тэмдэг нэр нь ХОЁР хүн эсвэл зүйлийг харьцуулдаг. БОГИНО тэмдэг нэр (1-2 үе): -ER + than нэм → tall→taller, smart→smarter. УРТ тэмдэг нэр (3+ үе): MORE + тэмдэг нэр + than → more beautiful, more intelligent. ДУР МЭДЭН хэлбэр: good→better, bad→worse, far→further.
commonMistakes: ❌ He is more tall than me.  ✅ He is TALLER than me.
❌ She is beautifuler.  ✅ She is MORE BEAUTIFUL.
❌ He is taller then me.  ✅ He is taller THAN me.
❌ This is more good.  ✅ This is BETTER.

page1.grammarTopic: Superlative adjectives — the tallest, the best, the most beautiful
page1.grammarExplanation: Superlative adjectives compare ONE thing to ALL others in a group. Always use THE before a superlative. SHORT adjectives: THE + adjective + EST → the tallest, the smallest, the biggest. LONG adjectives (3+ syllables): THE MOST + adjective → the most beautiful, the most expensive, the most popular. IRREGULAR: good→the best, bad→the worst, far→the furthest.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Superlative тэмдэг нэр нь нэг зүйлийг БҮГДТЭЙ харьцуулдаг. Superlative-ийн өмнө ҮРГЭЛЖ THE хэрэглэнэ. БОГИНО тэмдэг нэр: THE + тэмдэг нэр + EST → the tallest, the biggest. УРТ тэмдэг нэр: THE MOST + тэмдэг нэр → the most beautiful. ДУР МЭДЭН: good→the best, bad→the worst, far→the furthest.
commonMistakes: ❌ He is most tall.  ✅ He is THE MOST TALL. → Actually: THE TALLEST!
❌ She is the more beautiful.  ✅ She is THE MOST BEAUTIFUL.
❌ This is best restaurant.  ✅ This is THE BEST restaurant.
❌ It is most biggest.  ✅ It is THE BIGGEST.

page1.grammarTopic: Too / Enough — expressing excess and sufficiency
page1.grammarExplanation: TOO means MORE THAN NEEDED (negative meaning, there is a problem): too hot, too tired, too expensive. It comes BEFORE adjectives. ENOUGH means THE RIGHT AMOUNT (neutral or positive): big enough, fast enough. It comes AFTER adjectives. NOT ENOUGH means LESS THAN NEEDED: not big enough, not fast enough. Pattern: too + adj + to + verb → 'The soup is too hot TO EAT.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: TOO нь ХЭРЭГЦЭЭНЭЭС ИХ (сөрөг утга, асуудал байгаа) гэсэн утгатай: too hot, too tired, too expensive. Тэмдэг нэрийн ӨМНӨ ирдэг. ENOUGH нь ЗОХИСТОЙ ХЭМЖЭЭг (саармаг эсвэл эерэг) илэрхийлдэг. Тэмдэг нэрийн ДАРАА ирдэг. NOT ENOUGH нь ХЭРЭГЦЭЭНД ХҮРЭХГҮЙ: not big enough. Хэлбэр: too + тэмдэг нэр + to + үйлүг.

page1.grammarTopic: Would rather / Prefer to — expressing preferences
page1.grammarExplanation: WOULD RATHER expresses preference between two options: 'I would rather STAY home than GO out.' = I prefer staying home. After 'would rather': BARE INFINITIVE (no 'to'). PREFER TO expresses general preference: 'I prefer STAYING home to GOING out.' or 'I prefer tea TO coffee.' Structure: prefer + -ing + to + -ing / prefer + noun + to + noun.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: WOULD RATHER нь хоёр зүйлийн хооронд давуу байдлыг илэрхийлдэг: 'I would rather STAY home than GO out.' = Гэртээ байхыг илүүд үздэг. Would rather-ийн дараа: үйл үгийн ҮНДСЭН ХЭЛБЭР ('to' хэрэггүй). PREFER TO: ерөнхий давуу байдал. prefer + -ing + to + -ing. Prefer + нэр + to + нэр.
commonMistakes: ❌ I'd rather to stay home.  ✅ I'd rather STAY home. (no 'to' after rather!)
❌ I prefer coffee than tea.  ✅ I prefer coffee TO tea.
❌ Would you rather to go?  ✅ Would you rather GO?
❌ I prefer to walk than drive.  ✅ I prefer WALKING TO DRIVING.

Good friendship is not about having the same interests — it is about trust, respect, and loyalty. They would both rather have each other as friends than anyone else in the world!

page1.grammarTopic: Adverbs — quickly, carefully, well, badly, hard
page1.grammarExplanation: Adverbs describe HOW an action is done. Most adverbs are formed by adding -LY to adjectives: quick→quickly, careful→carefully, slow→slowly. IRREGULAR adverbs: good→well, fast→fast (same!), hard→hard (same!), early→early (same!). Position: adverbs usually come AFTER the verb or AFTER the object: 'She speaks English WELL.' 'He drives CAREFULLY.' Never between verb and object!
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Тодотгол үгс нь үйлдлийг ЯАГАН хийснийг тайлбарладаг. Ихэнх тодотгол үгс тэмдэг нэрт -LY нэмж үүсдэг: quick→quickly, careful→carefully. ДУР МЭДЭН хэлбэр: good→well, fast→fast (өөрчлөгдөхгүй!), hard→hard. Байрлал: тодотгол үг ихэвчлэн үйл үгийн ДАРАА эсвэл объектын ДАРАА ирдэг.
commonMistakes: ❌ She speaks English good.  ✅ She speaks English WELL.
❌ He works hardly.  ✅ He works HARD. (hard is already an adverb)
❌ She quickly speaks.  ✅ She speaks QUICKLY. (after the verb)
❌ He drives careful.  ✅ He drives CAREFULLY.

page1.grammarTopic: I think / I believe / In my opinion — expressing and defending views
page1.grammarExplanation: To express opinions in English, use phrases like: 'I think...', 'I believe...', 'In my opinion...', 'Personally...', 'I feel...'. To AGREE: 'I agree!', 'That's a good point.', 'Absolutely!', 'Exactly!'. To DISAGREE politely: 'I'm not sure about that.', 'I see your point, but...', 'Actually, I think...'. Always give a REASON after your opinion using 'because', 'for example', 'such as'.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Англиар санал бодлоо илэрхийлэхдээ: 'I think...', 'I believe...', 'In my opinion...', 'Personally...' зэрэг хэллэг хэрэглэнэ. ЗӨВШӨӨРӨХДӨӨ: 'I agree!', 'That's a good point.', 'Absolutely!'. ЗӨВШӨӨРӨХГҮЙ ЗОХИСТОЙГООР: 'I'm not sure about that.', 'I see your point, but...'. Санал бодлынхоо дараа ШАЛТГААН заавал нэм — 'because', 'for example'.

page1.grammarExplanation: Week 6 FULL REVIEW: 1) Comparatives: taller than / more beautiful than / better than. 2) Superlatives: the tallest / the most beautiful / the best. 3) Too/Enough: too hot / not big enough / too many people. 4) Would rather/Prefer: I'd rather stay / I prefer tea to coffee. 5) Adverbs: she speaks well / he works hard / she drives carefully. 6) Opinions: I think / I believe / In my opinion + because + example.

page1.grammarTopic: Reflexive pronouns — myself, yourself, himself, herself, ourselves, themselves
page1.grammarExplanation: Reflexive pronouns are used when the SUBJECT and OBJECT are the SAME person. 'I hurt MYSELF.' (I hurt me — same person). Also used for EMPHASIS: 'I made this MYSELF.' (nobody helped me, I did it alone — this stresses independence). Forms: I→myself, you→yourself, he→himself, she→herself, we→ourselves, they→themselves. Common phrases: by myself (alone), help yourself (take what you want), enjoy yourself (have a good time).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Reflexive pronoun нь субьект ба объект ИЖИЛ хүн байх үед хэрэглэгддэг. 'I hurt MYSELF.' (Би өөртөө гэмтлээ). Мөн ОНЦОЛЖ хэлэхэд хэрэглэнэ: 'I made this MYSELF.' (хэн ч тусалсангүй). Хэлбэрүүд: I→myself, you→yourself, he→himself, she→herself, we→ourselves, they→themselves. Нийтлэг хэллэгүүд: by myself (ганцаараа), help yourself (авч ид), enjoy yourself (хөгжилдөх).
commonMistakes: ❌ She hurt her.  ✅ She hurt HERSELF. (same person!)
❌ I made it by me.  ✅ I made it by MYSELF.
❌ He fixed it hisself.  ✅ He fixed it HIMSELF.
❌ They enjoyed theirselves.  ✅ They enjoyed THEMSELVES.

page1.grammarTopic: Should / Shouldn't — giving advice and recommendations
page1.grammarExplanation: SHOULD expresses ADVICE, RECOMMENDATIONS, and what is the right thing to do. It is LESS STRONG than 'must' or 'have to'. Form: subject + SHOULD + BASE VERB (no 'to'!). Negative: SHOULDN'T + base verb. Question: SHOULD + subject + base verb? Common uses: giving health advice, making recommendations, saying what is the right thing to do. Should = 'it is a good idea to...' Shouldn't = 'it is a bad idea to...'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: SHOULD нь ЗӨВЛӨГӨӨ, САНАЛ, зөв зүйлийг илэрхийлдэг. 'Must' болон 'have to'-оос ХҮЧИН ЧАДАЛ НЬ БАГА. Хэлбэр: субьект + SHOULD + ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ ('to' хэрэггүй!). Үгүйсгэл: SHOULDN'T + үндсэн үйлүг. Хэрэглэх тохиолдол: эрүүл мэндийн зөвлөгөө, санал болгох, зөв зүйлийг хэлэх. Should = 'хийх нь сайн санаа' · Shouldn't = 'хийхгүй байх нь сайн санаа'
commonMistakes: ❌ You should to exercise.  ✅ You should EXERCISE. (no 'to'!)
❌ You shouldn't to eat junk food.  ✅ You shouldn't EAT junk food.
❌ He should exercises.  ✅ He should EXERCISE. (base verb, no -s!)
❌ Should you eat more?  ✅ SHOULD you eat more?

page1.grammarTopic: Some / Any / A lot of — countable and uncountable quantities
page1.grammarExplanation: SOME is used in POSITIVE sentences and OFFERS/REQUESTS: 'I have SOME eggs.' 'Would you like SOME tea?' ANY is used in NEGATIVE sentences and QUESTIONS: 'I don't have ANY milk.' 'Do you have ANY eggs?' A LOT OF / LOTS OF works with BOTH countable and uncountable nouns: 'a lot of eggs / a lot of milk'. A FEW (countable) vs A LITTLE (uncountable): 'a few eggs' / 'a little milk'.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: SOME — ЭЕРЭГ өгүүлбэр болон санал/хүсэлтэд: 'I have SOME eggs.' 'Would you like SOME tea?' ANY — ҮГҮЙСГЭЛ ба АСУУЛТ: 'I don't have ANY milk.' 'Do you have ANY eggs?' A LOT OF / LOTS OF — хоёуланд нь: 'a lot of eggs / a lot of milk'. A FEW (тоолж болох) vs A LITTLE (тоолшгүй): 'a few eggs' / 'a little milk'.
commonMistakes: ❌ I have any eggs.  ✅ I have SOME eggs. (positive→some)
❌ Do you have some milk? (question)  ✅ Do you have ANY milk?
❌ I have a few milk.  ✅ I have A LITTLE milk. (uncountable!)
❌ Add a little eggs.  ✅ Add A FEW eggs. (countable!)

page1.grammarTopic: Too much / Too many / Not enough — quantity problems
page1.grammarExplanation: TOO MUCH + uncountable noun (problem): 'There is too much noise.' TOO MANY + countable noun (problem): 'There are too many people.' NOT ENOUGH + noun (insufficient): 'I don't have enough money.' These three structures all express PROBLEMS with quantities. Compare: too much noise (you can't count noise) vs too many people (you can count people: 1 person, 2 people). NOT ENOUGH can follow with 'to + verb': 'Not enough money TO buy it.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: TOO MUCH + тоолшгүй нэр (асуудал): 'There is too much noise.' TOO MANY + тоолж болох нэр (асуудал): 'There are too many people.' NOT ENOUGH + нэр (хангалтгүй): 'I don't have enough money.' Эдгээр гурав нь хэмжээний АСУУДЛЫГ илэрхийлдэг. Харьцуул: too much noise (чимээг тоолж болохгүй) vs too many people (хүнийг тоолж болно). NOT ENOUGH + to + verb: 'Not enough money TO buy it.'
commonMistakes: ❌ Too many noise.  ✅ TOO MUCH noise. (uncountable!)
❌ Too much people.  ✅ TOO MANY people. (countable!)
❌ Not enough of money.  ✅ Not enough money. (no 'of'!)
❌ There are too much clothes.  ✅ There are TOO MANY clothes.

page1.grammarTopic: First Conditional — If I have money, I will buy...
page1.grammarExplanation: The FIRST CONDITIONAL expresses REAL and POSSIBLE future situations. Structure: IF + Present Simple, WILL + base verb. The IF clause describes a possible condition; the WILL clause describes the likely result. 'If I save money, I will travel to Korea.' The IF clause can come first or second: 'I will travel to Korea IF I save money.' Both mean the same thing! Note: always use PRESENT SIMPLE after 'if' in First Conditional — NEVER 'will' after 'if'.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: FIRST CONDITIONAL нь БОДИТ болон БОЛОМЖТОЙ ирээдүйн нөхцлийг илэрхийлдэг. Хэлбэр: IF + Present Simple, WILL + үндсэн үйл үг. IF хэсэг нь боломжтой нөхцлийг, WILL хэсэг нь тохиолдлын үр дүнг илэрхийлдэг. 'If I save money, I will travel to Korea.' АНХААРУУЛГА: 'if'-ийн дараа ХЭЗЭЭ Ч 'will' хэрэглэдэггүй — ҮРГЭЛЖ Present Simple!
commonMistakes: ❌ If I will save money, I will travel.  ✅ If I SAVE money, I will travel.
❌ If I will work hard, I will earn more.  ✅ If I WORK hard, I will earn more.
❌ I will travel if I will have money.  ✅ I will travel if I HAVE money.
❌ If I save, I would travel.  ✅ If I save, I WILL travel. (1st conditional!)

page1.grammarTopic: Second Conditional — If I were rich, I would buy...
page1.grammarExplanation: The SECOND CONDITIONAL expresses UNREAL, IMAGINARY, or UNLIKELY situations in the present or future. Structure: IF + Past Simple, WOULD + base verb. 'If I were rich, I would travel the world.' (I am NOT rich — this is imaginary.) KEY: Use WERE for all persons with 'to be' (not 'was'): 'If I WERE you...' 'If she WERE here...' This is different from First Conditional (real/possible) → Second Conditional (unreal/imaginary).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: SECOND CONDITIONAL нь БОДИТ БУС, ТӨСӨӨЛЛИЙН нөхцлийг илэрхийлдэг. Хэлбэр: IF + Past Simple, WOULD + үндсэн үйл үг. 'If I were rich, I would travel.' (Би баян биш — энэ бол төсөөлөл.) ЧУХАЛ: 'to be'-ийн хувьд бүх нэрт WERE хэрэглэнэ: 'If I WERE you...' 'If she WERE here...' First Conditional (бодит/боломжтой) ≠ Second Conditional (бодит бус/төсөөлөл).

page1.grammarTopic: Week 7 review — reflexive pronouns, should, some/any, conditionals
page1.grammarExplanation: WEEK 7 REVIEW: 1) Reflexive pronouns: myself/himself/herself/ourselves/themselves — same person as subject or for emphasis. 2) Should/Shouldn't: advice and recommendations. 3) Some/Any: some=positive+offers, any=negative+questions. 4) Too much/Too many/Not enough: quantity problems. 5) FIRST Conditional: real future — IF + Present Simple + WILL. 6) SECOND Conditional: unreal/imaginary — IF + Past Simple + WOULD. Key difference: 1st=possible, 2nd=imaginary!
page1.grammarExplanationMn: 7-Р ДОЛОО ХОНОГИЙН БҮРЭН ДАВТАЛТ: 1) Reflexive pronouns: myself/himself/herself — субьекттай ижил хүн эсвэл онцолж хэлэхэд. 2) Should/Shouldn't: зөвлөгөө. 3) Some/Any: some=эерэг+санал, any=үгүйсгэл+асуулт. 4) Too much/many/Not enough: хэмжээний асуудал. 5) FIRST Conditional: бодит — IF+Present Simple+WILL. 6) SECOND Conditional: бодит бус — IF+Past Simple+WOULD.

page1.grammarTopic: Must / Mustn't / Have to — obligation and prohibition
page1.grammarExplanation: MUST expresses STRONG OBLIGATION or NECESSITY (often from the speaker or a rule): 'You must arrive on time.' MUSTN'T expresses PROHIBITION (something is not allowed): 'You mustn't be late.' HAVE TO also expresses obligation but comes from EXTERNAL rules or circumstances: 'I have to wear a uniform.' (company rule). DON'T HAVE TO means NOT NECESSARY (but you can if you want): 'You don't have to work weekends.' Key: MUSTN'T = prohibited (don't do it!) ≠ DON'T HAVE TO = not necessary (you can choose).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: MUST нь ХҮЧТЭЙ ҮҮРЭГ эсвэл ЗАЙЛШГҮЙ байдлыг илэрхийлдэг (ихэвчлэн яригчаас эсвэл дүрмээс): 'You must arrive on time.' MUSTN'T нь ХОРИГЛОЛТЫГ илэрхийлдэг: 'You mustn't be late.' HAVE TO мөн үүргийг илэрхийлэх боловч ГАДНААС ирдэг: 'I have to wear a uniform.' (компанийн дүрэм). DON'T HAVE TO = ХИЙХ ШААРДЛАГАГҮЙ (хийж болно). ЧУХАЛ: MUSTN'T = ХОРИГЛОГДСОН (хийж болохгүй!) ≠ DON'T HAVE TO = шаардлагагүй.
commonMistakes: ❌ You mustn't to arrive late.  ✅ You mustn't ARRIVE late. (no 'to'!)
❌ I must to wear a uniform.  ✅ I must WEAR a uniform.
❌ You don't have to smoke = it's not necessary  ✅ You MUSTN'T smoke = it's PROHIBITED!
❌ She have to work.  ✅ She HAS TO work. (3rd person → has to)

page1.grammarTopic: Need to / Don't need to — necessity vs no necessity
page1.grammarExplanation: NEED TO expresses NECESSITY — something that is required or important to do. It is similar to 'have to' but often refers to practical necessity. 'I need to finish this report.' (it is necessary for me to finish it). DON'T NEED TO means something is NOT NECESSARY — you have a choice. 'You don't need to come early.' (it's not required — but you can if you want). Negative of 'need to' ≠ mustn't! Don't need to = optional. Mustn't = prohibited.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: NEED TO нь ЗАЙЛШГҮЙ БАЙДЛЫГ илэрхийлдэг — хийх шаардлагатай зүйл. 'I need to finish this report.' (дуусгах шаардлагатай). DON'T NEED TO нь ШААРДЛАГАГҮЙ гэсэн утгатай — чи сонгох эрхтэй. 'You don't need to come early.' (шаардлагагүй — гэхдээ хүсвэл ирж болно). DON'T NEED TO ≠ MUSTN'T! Don't need to = сонголттой. Mustn't = хориглогдсон.
commonMistakes: ❌ I need submit this.  ✅ I need TO submit this. (need to + base verb)
❌ She need to attend.  ✅ She NEEDS TO attend. (3rd person -s!)
❌ You don't need to smoke here. (wrong meaning!)  ✅ You MUSTN'T smoke here. (if prohibited)
❌ I needed to submitting.  ✅ I needed TO SUBMIT. (base verb after need to)

page1.grammarTopic: Future Simple — will / won't for predictions and decisions
page1.grammarExplanation: WILL is used for: 1) PREDICTIONS about the future (what we think will happen): 'English will be even more important in 50 years.' 2) SPONTANEOUS DECISIONS made at the moment of speaking: 'I'll help you with that!' (decided right now). 3) PROMISES: 'I won't give up!' 4) OFFERS: 'I'll carry that for you.' Compare with GOING TO (already planned): 'I'm going to study engineering.' (decided before). WILL = prediction/spontaneous/promise/offer.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: WILL нь дараах тохиолдолд хэрэглэгддэг: 1) ИРЭЭДҮЙН ТААМАГЛАЛ: 'English will be more important in 50 years.' 2) ТЭР МӨЧИД ГАРСАН ШИЙДВЭР: 'I'll help you!' (одоо шийдсэн). 3) АМЛАЛТ: 'I won't give up!' 4) САНАЛ: 'I'll carry that.' Харьцуул GOING TO (урьдаас төлөвлөсөн): 'I'm going to study engineering.' (өмнө шийдсэн). WILL = таамаглал/тэр мөчийн шийдвэр/амлалт/санал.

page1.grammarTopic: Will vs Going to — the key difference
page1.grammarExplanation: KEY DIFFERENCE between WILL and GOING TO: GOING TO = 1) Already decided plans: 'I'm going to study medicine.' (decided before speaking) 2) Predictions based on evidence/what you can see: 'Look at those clouds — it's going to rain!' WILL = 1) Spontaneous decisions made right now: 'The phone is ringing — I'll answer it!' 2) Predictions without evidence (opinions): 'I think English will be important in 50 years.' 3) Promises and offers: 'I'll help you.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: WILL ба GOING TO-ийн ГОЛ ЯЛГАА: GOING TO = 1) Урьдаас шийдсэн төлөвлөгөө: 'I'm going to study medicine.' 2) Нотолгоо дээр суурилсан таамаглал: 'Look at those clouds — it's going to rain!' WILL = 1) Тэр мөчид гарсан шийдвэр: 'I'll answer it!' 2) Нотолгоогүй таамаглал (бодол): 'I think English will be important.' 3) Амлалт ба санал.
commonMistakes: ❌ I'll study medicine next year. (if already planned)  ✅ I'M GOING TO study medicine.
❌ It's going to be important in 50 years. (opinion, no evidence)  ✅ It WILL be important.
❌ Look at those clouds — it will rain!  ✅ It's GOING TO rain! (visible evidence)
❌ The phone rings — I'm going to answer!  ✅ I'LL answer! (spontaneous decision now)

page1.grammarTopic: Present Passive — is made / is used / is called / is known
page1.grammarExplanation: The PASSIVE VOICE moves the focus from the DOER of the action to the RECEIVER. ACTIVE: 'Samsung makes this phone.' (focus on Samsung — the doer). PASSIVE: 'This phone is made by Samsung.' (focus on the phone — the receiver). PRESENT PASSIVE form: is/are + past participle. Use passive when: 1) The doer is unknown or unimportant. 2) The action is more important than who did it. 3) In formal/scientific writing. 'by + agent' is optional — often left out when unknown.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ИДЭВХГҮЙ ХЭЛБЭР нь үйлийг ХИЙГЧ-ийн оронд ХҮЛЭЭН АВАГЧ дээр анхаарлыг чиглүүлдэг. ИДЭВХТЭЙ: 'Samsung makes this phone.' (Samsung дээр анхаарна). ИДЭВХГҮЙ: 'This phone is made by Samsung.' (утас дээр анхаарна). ОДОО ЦАГИЙН ИДЭВХГҮЙ ХЭЛБЭР: is/are + past participle. Хэзээ хэрэглэх: 1) Хийгч тодорхойгүй. 2) Үйл нь чухал. 3) Албан ёсны/шинжлэх ухааны бичиглэлд.

page1.grammarTopic: Past Passive — was built / was invented / was discovered
page1.grammarExplanation: The PAST PASSIVE is formed with WAS/WERE + PAST PARTICIPLE. Use it to describe historical events and facts where the focus is on WHAT happened, not on WHO did it. WAS = singular subjects: 'The telephone WAS INVENTED by Bell.' WERE = plural subjects: 'Many cities WERE BUILT along the Silk Road.' Same rules as present passive: use 'by + agent' when the doer is important. Leave out 'by' when the doer is unknown or unimportant.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГИЙН ИДЭВХГҮЙ ХЭЛБЭР: WAS/WERE + PAST PARTICIPLE. Түүхэн үйл явдал, баримтуудыг тайлбарлахад хэрэглэдэг — ХЭН хийснийг биш, ЮУ болсон гэдгийг онцолдог. WAS = нэгдмэл: 'The telephone WAS INVENTED.' WERE = олон тоо: 'Cities WERE BUILT.' 'by + agent' — хийгч чухал үед хэрэглэнэ. Тодорхойгүй үед 'by'-г орхиж болно.
commonMistakes: ❌ The phone was invent in 1876.  ✅ The phone was INVENTED in 1876. (past participle!)
❌ Many cities was built.  ✅ Many cities WERE built. (plural → were)
❌ It was invented from Bell.  ✅ It was invented BY Bell. (use 'by' not 'from'!)
❌ Where was Genghis Khan borned?  ✅ Where was Genghis Khan BORN? (irregular — born, not borned!)

page1.grammarTopic: Modals + Passive + Future — Week 8 complete review
page1.grammarExplanation: WEEK 8 FULL REVIEW: 1) Need to / Don't need to = necessity vs not necessary. 2) Will = prediction, spontaneous decision, promise, offer. 3) Going to = already planned, based on visible evidence. 4) Present Passive = is/are + past participle. 5) Past Passive = was/were + past participle. KEY CONTRASTS: mustn't (PROHIBITED) ≠ don't need to (OPTIONAL). Will (spontaneous/opinion) ≠ going to (planned/evidence). Active (who does it?) vs Passive (what happens?).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: 8-Р ДОЛОО ХОНОГИЙН БҮРЭН ДАВТАЛТ: 1) Need to / Don't need to = шаардлага vs шаардлагагүй. 2) Will = таамаглал, тэр мөчийн шийдвэр, амлалт, санал. 3) Going to = урьдаас шийдсэн / нотолгоо дээр суурилсан. 4) Present Passive = is/are + past participle. 5) Past Passive = was/were + past participle. ГОЛ ЯЛГАА: mustn't ≠ don't need to. Will ≠ going to. Active vs Passive.
commonMistakes: ❌ You don't need to smoke here. (wrong — it's prohibited!)  ✅ You MUSTN'T smoke here.
❌ I'll go to Korea next year. (if already planned)  ✅ I'M GOING TO go to Korea.
❌ The phone is make in China.  ✅ The phone IS MADE in China.
❌ It was invent in 1876.  ✅ It was INVENTED in 1876.

page1.grammarTopic: Reported speech — She said that... / He told me that...
page1.grammarExplanation: REPORTED SPEECH (indirect speech) tells us what someone SAID without using their exact words. KEY CHANGES when reporting: 1) Verb tense shifts back: am→was, is→was, are→were, have→had, will→would, can→could. 2) Pronouns change: I→he/she, we→they, my→his/her. Direct: 'I am tired.' → Reported: She said (that) she WAS tired. Direct: 'I will help.' → Reported: He said he WOULD help. 'That' is optional after 'said' and 'told'.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ӨГҮҮЛЛЭГИЙН ЯРИ (indirect speech) нь хэн нэгний хэлсэн зүйлийг ЯГ ТЭР ҮГЭЭР нь хэлэлгүй дамжуулна. ГОЛ ӨӨРЧЛӨЛТҮҮД: 1) Цаг нь ухарна: am→was, is→was, will→would, can→could, have→had. 2) Төлөөний үг өөрчлөгдөнө: I→he/she, we→they. Шууд: 'I am tired.' → Өгүүллэг: She said she WAS tired. 'That' нь заавал биш.
commonMistakes: ❌ She said me she was tired.  ✅ She TOLD ME she was tired. (told needs object!)
❌ He said that he is tired.  ✅ He said that he WAS tired. (tense shift!)
❌ She told that she was ready.  ✅ She TOLD ME that she was ready. (tell needs object!)
❌ He said he would to help.  ✅ He said he WOULD help. (no 'to' after would!)

page1.grammarTopic: Agreeing and disagreeing — polite discussion phrases
page1.grammarExplanation: Agreeing and disagreeing politely is an essential A2+ conversation skill. AGREE: 'I completely agree!' · 'Absolutely!' · 'Exactly!' · 'That's a great point.' · 'You're absolutely right.' PARTIALLY AGREE: 'I see your point, but...' · 'To be fair...' · 'You have a point, however...' DISAGREE politely: 'I'm not sure about that.' · 'Actually, I think...' · 'On the other hand...' · 'I respectfully disagree.' Always give a REASON after agreeing or disagreeing!
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Зохистойгоор зөвшөөрөх ба зөвшөөрөхгүй байх нь A2+ яриа хэлэлцлийн чухал ур чадвар. ЗӨВШӨӨРӨХ: 'I completely agree!' · 'Absolutely!' · 'Exactly!' ХЭСЭГЧЛЭН ЗӨВШӨӨРӨХ: 'I see your point, but...' · 'To be fair...' ЗОХИСТОЙГООР ЗӨВШӨӨРӨХГҮЙ: 'I'm not sure about that.' · 'Actually, I think...' · 'On the other hand...' Зөвшөөрсөн, зөвшөөрөхгүй байсан ч ШАЛТГААН ЗААВАЛ НЭМ!

page1.grammarTopic: Relative clauses — who, which, that
page1.grammarExplanation: RELATIVE CLAUSES add extra information about a noun using WHO, WHICH, or THAT. WHO = for people: 'The student WHO studies hard will succeed.' WHICH = for things: 'The book WHICH I read was amazing.' THAT = for both people and things (informal): 'The film THAT we watched was great.' WHERE = for places: 'The library WHERE I study is quiet.' WHOSE = for possession: 'The artist WHOSE work I love is Sarnai.' Relative clauses join two sentences: 'I know a man. He speaks 5 languages.' → 'I know a man WHO speaks 5 languages.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ХАРЬЦААНЫ ӨГҮҮЛБЭРИЙН ХЭСЭГ (relative clause) нь нэрийн тухай WHO, WHICH, THAT ашиглан нэмэлт мэдээлэл өгдөг. WHO = хүний тухай: 'The student WHO studies hard.' WHICH = зүйлийн тухай: 'The book WHICH I read.' THAT = хоёуланд (хэвийн ярианд): 'The film THAT we watched.' WHERE = газрын тухай: 'The library WHERE I study.' Relative clause нь хоёр өгүүлбэрийг нэгтгэдэг!
commonMistakes: ❌ The book who I read was great.  ✅ The book WHICH I read was great. (book=thing→which!)
❌ The man which helped me was kind.  ✅ The man WHO helped me was kind. (man=person→who!)
❌ The place who I study is quiet.  ✅ The place WHERE I study is quiet. (place→where!)
❌ The author which I like writes well.  ✅ The author WHO I like writes well. (author=person→who!)

In 60 days you mastered: Storytelling sequences · Present Perfect · For/Since · Comparative/Superlative adjectives · Too/Enough · Would rather/Prefer · Adverbs · Opinion phrases · Reflexive pronouns · Should/Shouldn't · Some/Any · Conditionals 1 & 2 · Must/Mustn't · Need to · Will vs Going to · Present & Past Passive · Reported Speech · Agreeing/Disagreeing · Relative Clauses. You can now have real conversations at A2 level. You are no longer a beginner — you are INTERMEDIATE!

page2.readingPassage: Day 60. A2 Graduation Day. Sixty days ago, Bat and Sarnai couldn't express complex ideas in English. Today is completely different. Bat has studied English consistently for two months — every single day. He can now compare things, describe his experiences, discuss opinions, report what others said, and use relative clauses naturally. He says: "If I had given up, I would never have reached this level." Sarnai adds: "English is spoken worldwide, which means our opportunities are now limitless." Their teacher says: "I told you that you would succeed — and you did! Congratulations! 🎓"

speakingQuestions: 1.  Introduce yourself completely at A2 level — name, background, studies, experiences, plans, opinions!
2.  What was the most useful grammar you learned in Book 2?
3.  Use a relative clause: describe someone who inspired you during these 60 days.
4.  Use reported speech: what did your teacher tell you during Book 2?
5.  If you hadn't studied English for 60 days, what would be different? (2nd Conditional!)
6.  What are you going to do next to continue improving? Make a real plan!

page3.listeningScript:
Teacher: Class — this is our A2 Graduation Day! How do you feel, Bat?
Bat: I feel incredibly proud! When I started Day 31, I could barely use Present Perfect. Now I can use passive voice, reported speech, and relative clauses!
Teacher: That is extraordinary progress! Sarnai — what has changed for you?
Sarnai: Everything! I can now express my opinions confidently, agree and disagree politely, and describe my art in English to international audiences.
Teacher: That is beautiful! You have both worked so consistently. What will you do next?
Bat: We are going to start Book 3 together! I told Sarnai that we wouldn't stop until we reached B1.
Sarnai: And I said that nothing could stop us now. The student who studies every day will always succeed!
Teacher: You are both extraordinary. I am so proud of you. Congratulations, class — you are officially A2! 🎓
